tata.FY2023-24: AMBIGUOUS: FY2022-23 is consolidated while later years are standalone

sections.about: This metric measures the share of direct sourcing from within the district and neighbouring districts, making it a local procurement and inclusive-development indicator under BRSR Principle 8. It indicates the extent to which plant-level procurement supports surrounding industrial ecosystems, service providers and community-linked suppliers. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers:
In FY2024-25, JSW led at 67.22%, followed by Tata at 63%, Jindal at 41% and SAIL at 31.36%. In FY2023-24, JSW also led at 65% after standardisation, while Tata was close at 64%, Jindal stood at 42% and SAIL was 0%. In FY2022-23, Tata led at 41%, followed by Jindal at 34.23%, JSW at 2.8% and SAIL at 0%.

The structural driver is plant geography and procurement perimeter. Tata’s Jamshedpur/Kalinganagar ecosystem, local services, maintenance, stores and downstream industrial clusters support a high local sourcing share, but bulk raw materials such as coking coal, iron ore, limestone and ferro alloys remain structurally non-local or captive. JSW’s 65% and 67.22% values indicate either strong local procurement design or a standardized conversion that captures a large district-neighbourhood spend base. Jindal’s 41–42% local share is healthy for stainless operations but constrained by alloying metals and scrap/ferroalloy supply chains. SAIL’s move from 0% to 31.36% in FY2025 is a major improvement but still leaves it last, likely reflecting the complexity of multi-plant public-sector procurement and centralised/captive sourcing.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranked 2nd at 63%, 4.22 percentage points behind JSW, or 6.3% below the leader on a relative basis. Tata’s rank declined from 1st in FY2023 to 2nd in FY2024 and FY2025, largely because JSW’s local sourcing jumped materially from 2.8% to 65% in FY2024 and then to 67.22% in FY2025. Tata’s own FY2024-to-FY2025 movement was marginally negative, from 64% to 63%, but the level remains strong for an integrated steelmaker with large cross-regional raw-material and logistics flows.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Tata Steel FY2022-23 is reported collectively for Tata Steel and its Indian subsidiaries; FY2023-24 and FY2024-25 are standalone.
